_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-leg batch (House HD-33/34/35 + Senate SD-34/35): monique-miller (HD-33 R freshman; FRTL 'A' + GOA + FL Family Action endorsed, iVoterGuide questionnaire pro-life/marriage/school-choice/2A, PRIME SPONSOR HB6025 emergency-firearm-restriction repeal signed-law + HB491 + HB759 co-sponsor; ~100), robbie-brackett (HD-34 R 2nd term [CORRECTION: elected 2022 not 2024-freshman]; thin retrievable record — HB759 co-sponsor + NRA-2022 + law-enforcement endorsement; ~100 on 2 cells), shevrin-jones (SD-34 D; CURRENCY: NOT seeking SD-34 reelection, running US House FL-24 2026; uniformly progressive — NAY SB300/HB5/HB543/HB931/SB7050, Equality FL + Planned Parenthood endorsed, EXCUSED on HB3/SB1718/SB4-C so no cross-party catch; 0), barbara-sharief (SD-35 D; CORRECTION: elected Nov-2024 term-to-2028 NOT on 2026 ballot, religion ISLAM per official page; could not vote on 2023-24 bills; only HARD roll call NAY SB4-C 2025 + EMILY's List 2021; 0), erika-booth (HD-35 R; FRTL 'A' + NRA-backed, YEA HB1517 personhood/HB759 guns/HB1009 religious-expression/HB1539 harmful-materials/HB443 charters/SB4-C immigration, opposes trans-education; ~100).

set.office: State Representative (HD-33 · freshman, elected Nov 2024; seeking reelection 2026)

profile.religion: Christian (self-described 'Biblical worldview'; denomination not publicly specified)
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-33 (Brevard County) Republican; assumed office Nov 5, 2024 (freshman); running for reelection (R primary Aug 18, 2026; general Nov 3, 2026). Cybersecurity executive; former Moms for Liberty advisory-board member. Scored from HARD bill sponsorships (signed law HB6025) + her cited iVoterGuide candidate-questionnaire self-statements + HARD single-issue endorsements (Florida Right to Life 'A', Gun Owners of America, Florida Family Action). NOTE on a 2A intra-base controversy: she publicly pledged to file an open-carry bill (backed by GOA) then declined to file it in 2025 ('isn't the right time'), drawing a GOA rebuke — a base grievance, NOT a vote against the pro-gun position, so not scored against her.
profile.background: Cybersecurity executive; former Moms for Liberty advisory-board member. Elected to the FL House (HD-33) in 2024.

monique-miller@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Solid conservative freshman; no cross-party deviation. HARD: Florida Right to Life 'A' rating (2024) + iVoterGuide questionnaire 'Foster a culture of LIFE'/opposes elective abortion & euthanasia (life q0,q3); questionnaire affirms marriage as 'union of one man and one woman' + opposes biological males in women's sports/taxpayer gender transition (marriage q0,q2); strongly supports school choice w/ funding-follows-the-child (family q0) + Moms for Liberty advisory board → opposes CRT/SOGI (family q2); opposes voting without photo ID + opposes no-excuse mail-in (election q1,q3); 2A is strongest — PRIME SPONSOR of HB6025 (2025, repeals automatic firearm/ammo restrictions during declared emergencies — passed House 86-28, signed into law May 28, 2025), sponsored HB491, co-sponsored HB759 (restore 18-20 purchase rights), endorsed by Gun Owners of America (self_defense q0,q1). Public-justice, economics, 10th-Amendment, faith-liberty-policy, and individual immigration roll calls returned no HARD evidence and are null (general 'border security' language too vague to score).

0.note: Florida Right to Life 'A' rating (2024) + iVoterGuide questionnaire: 'Foster a culture of LIFE,' opposes elective abortion and taxpayer-funded abortion providers — affirms a pro-life/personhood stance

3.note: iVoterGuide questionnaire: opposes euthanasia / physician-assisted suicide

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

0.note: iVoterGuide questionnaire: defines marriage as 'a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman'

1.note: No discrete same-sex-marriage/civil-union legal-recognition position isolated

2.note: iVoterGuide: opposes biological males in women's sports/spaces + opposes taxpayer-funded gender transition — affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology in policy

0.note: iVoterGuide: strongly supports school choice (public/private/homeschool) with funding following the child

1.note: General 'parental decision-making rights' language too broad to score the medical/gender-consent cell — null

2.note: Former Moms for Liberty advisory-board member — affirmative opposition to CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology curricula in K-12

3.note: No porn-age-verification position located (HB3 predates her tenure)

0.note: Personal 'Biblical worldview' is biographical, not a policy position on public profession — null

1.note: iVoterGuide: opposes voting without photo ID — supports photo voter ID

3.note: iVoterGuide: opposes no-excuse mail-in voting — opposes mass mail-in

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar located
Campaign 'enhanced southern border security' too general to score the barrier/military cell — null

2.note: No isolated sanctuary-policy vote (HB3-era bills predate her); not inferred

0.note: iVoterGuide: supports no restrictions on gun ownership for law-abiding citizens + endorsed by Gun Owners of America; publicly championed open carry — supports constitutional carry

1.note: PRIME SPONSOR HB6025 (2025) repealing automatic firearm/ammo restrictions during declared emergencies (signed into law) + co-sponsor HB759 (restore 18-20 purchase rights) — opposes gun-control restrictions

set.office: State Representative (HD-34 · 2nd term, first elected 2022; seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-34 (Indian River County / Vero Beach) Republican; CORRECTION to prior record — first elected to the FL House in 2022 (not a 2024 freshman), reelected 2024, now seeking a third term (R primary Aug 18, 2026). Former Mayor of Vero Beach. THIN RETRIEVABLE RECORD: flhouse.gov sponsored-bills/roll-call PDFs are JavaScript-rendered and were not machine-readable this pass, and his campaign Issues page is limited to water/fiscal/local-control (silent on most social issues), so most cells are honestly null rather than party-inferred. Scored only the HARD facts located. Religion 'Christian' per his flhouse.gov profile.
profile.background: Former Mayor of Vero Beach. Elected to the FL House (HD-34) in 2022; sits on the PreK-12 Budget and Criminal Justice subcommittees.

robbie-brackett@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (thin — most individual roll calls not retrievable; scored only HARD facts, no party inference). HARD: co-introducer of HB759 (2025) restoring firearm-purchase rights for ages 18-20 (House passed 78-34, Mar 26, 2025) + NRA endorsement as a 2022 House candidate (self_defense q1); endorsed by Indian River County first responders / law enforcement and co-introduced HB929 (2025 firefighter health & safety) — pro-law-enforcement alignment (public_justice q0). Life, marriage, family, christian-liberty, economics, elections, 10th-Amendment, and immigration individual votes were NOT independently retrievable (HB289/HB3/HB931/SB4-C roll-call PDFs not machine-readable) and are null per the cardinal rule — not inferred from party.

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

3.note: HB3 individual vote not retrievable — null, not inferred
HB931 chaplains individual vote not retrievable — null

2.note: SB4-C individual vote not retrievable — null, not inferred

set.office: State Senator (SD-34 · term to 2026; not seeking SD-34 reelection — running for higher office in 2026)

profile.religion: Christian (nondenominational) — per official flsenate.gov member page
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-34 (Broward/Miami-Dade) Democrat; FL House 2012-2020, Senate since 2020 (SD-35, redistricted to SD-34 in 2022); Florida's first openly LGBTQ state senator. CURRENCY: announced May 27, 2026 he will NOT seek SD-34 reelection (term ends Nov 2026) and has filed to run for U.S. House FL-24 (succeeding retiring Rep. Frederica Wilson) — SD-34 is an OPEN seat in 2026 and Jones is not its candidate; status set running_higher_office. Scored from HARD Senate roll-call PDFs. NOTE: he was EXCUSED (not a recorded vote) on HB3, SB1718, and SB4-C — so the high-value Democrat-crossover catch is absent (he cast no conservative-direction vote). Religion 'Christian (nondenominational)' per flsenate.gov.
profile.background: FL House 2012-2020 (HD-101); FL Senate since 2020. First openly LGBTQ Florida state senator. Filed for U.S. House FL-24 in 2026.

shevrin-jones@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Uniformly progressive Broward/Miami-Dade Democrat; no cross-party deviation. HARD: NAY SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban) + NAY HB5 (2022 15-week ban) (life q0); Planned Parenthood (FAPPA) PAC + Equality Florida PAC endorsements (life q4, marriage q0/q1); opposed HB1521 (2023 trans-bathroom) and publicly condemned HB1521/HB1069/SB254/SB1438 (marriage q2,q4); NAY HB931 (2024 school chaplains) (christian_liberty q0); NAY SB7050 (2023 elections omnibus) (election q3); NAY HB543 (2023 permitless carry) (self_defense q0). EXCUSED (NOT a vote) on HB3 (2024), SB1718 (2023), SB4-C (2025) — recorded as null, not opposition. Economics, public-justice, and 10th-Amendment cells returned no individual evidence and are null.

0.note: NAY SB300 (2023 6-week ban) + NAY HB5 (2022 15-week ban) — rejects personhood-from-conception protection

4.note: Publicly condemned HB1069 'Don't Say Gay' expansion — supports LGBTQ identity in schools/public policy

set.office: State Senator (SD-35 · elected Nov 2024, term to 2028; not on the 2026 ballot)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-35 (Broward) Democrat; CORRECTION to prior record — assumed office Nov 5, 2024 (defeating R Vincent Parlatore, succeeding term-limited Sen. Lauren Book), 4-year term to Nov 2028 — NOT on the 2026 ballot (candidacy_status set to incumbent, not seeking-reelection). Former Broward County Commissioner / Broward Mayor; nurse/businesswoman. CRITICAL: because she was sworn in Nov 2024, she COULD NOT have voted on the 2023-2024 marquee bills (SB300/HB1521/HB3/HB931/HB543/SB1718/SB7050) — those cells are null (no vote exists), not party-inferred. Her first session was 2025. Religion 'Islam' per her official flsenate.gov member page (a primary source). 2025 regular-session roll calls were largely not retrievable this pass — best avenue for additional HARD evidence next.
profile.background: Former Broward County Commissioner and Broward Mayor; nurse and home-health-care business owner. Elected to the FL Senate (SD-35) in 2024.

barbara-sharief@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (thin — first session 2025; predates the 2023-24 marquee votes). No cross-party deviation. HARD: NAY SB4-C (2025 special-session illegal-immigration crackdown; Senate passed 25-11 Feb 13, 2025) — opposed the enforcement/anti-sanctuary measure (border q2) and argued on the floor against applying the death penalty based on immigration status; EMILY's List endorsement (2021, for the CD-20 congressional special — different race, but documents accepting abortion-industry-aligned backing) (life q4). All 2023-2024 marquee-bill cells are null because she was not yet a member (no vote exists). Marriage/family/christian-liberty/economics/elections/2A/10th-Amendment returned no in-tenure HARD evidence and are null.

set.office: State Representative (HD-35 · elected Nov 2024; seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-35 (Osceola/Polk — St. Cloud) Republican; LOST the Jan 16, 2024 special election to incumbent D Tom Keen, then WON the rematch (GOP primary Aug 20, 2024 w/ DeSantis endorsement; general Nov 5, 2024). Seeking reelection (R primary Aug 18, 2026; general vs D Eric Gray). 20-year public elementary teacher; former Osceola County School Board member. Scored from HARD 2025-session roll calls (per the Digital Democracy / Plural per-legislator vote records + bill pages) + FRTL 'A' + NRA-PVF backing + cited campaign statements. Religion/denomination not stated by any primary source — left unset, not inferred.
profile.background: Twenty-year public elementary-school teacher; former Osceola County School Board member (2022-2023). Elected to the FL House (HD-35) in 2024 after losing a Jan-2024 special and winning the November rematch.

erika-booth@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Solid conservative; no cross-party deviation (every located vote breaks right). HARD: YEA HB1517 (2025 civil liability for wrongful death of an unborn child / personhood; House passed 79-32) + Florida Right to Life 'A' (2024) (life q0); YEA HB1009 (2025 Religious & Patriotic Expression in Public Schools incl. 'In God We Trust') (christian_liberty q0,q3); YEA HB443 (2025 charter schools) → school choice (family q0); campaign opposes 'trans-education in our public schools' + YEA HB1539 (2025 materials harmful to minors) (family q2,q3); YEA SB4-C (2025 immigration / criminalize unauthorized entry) (border q2); YEA HB759 (2025 lowering long-gun purchase age to 18; House 78-34) + NRA Political Victory Fund backing (self_defense q1). Economics, public-justice, 10th-Amendment, and specific election-bill cells returned no HARD evidence (campaign 'audit elections/root out fraud' too general) and are null.
